title: P6: Kubernetes Manifests
description:
K8s manifests for deploying to apexalgo-iad cluster.
- Namespace: domain-check
- Deployment: 1 replica (can scale), resource limits (128Mi mem, 100m CPU)
- Service: ClusterIP on port 8080
- ConfigMap: DOMCHECK_ env vars (cache size, TTLs, trust-proxy=true, log-format=json)
- Liveness probe: GET /health, initialDelaySeconds: 5
- Readiness probe: GET /health, periodSeconds: 10
- SecurityContext: non-root, read-only filesystem
Directory: cluster-configuration/apexalgo-iad/domain-check/

title: T1: Unit Tests — Bootstrap, Cache, Rate Limiter
description:
Unit tests for supporting infrastructure:
Bootstrap: real + malformed JSON parsing, TLD lookup, fallback behavior
Cache: TTL expiration, LRU eviction at max capacity, concurrent access, hit/miss tracking
Rate Limiter: token bucket behavior, per-IP isolation, stale entry eviction
Package: internal/checker/*_test.go

title: P5: README + Documentation
description:
Comprehensive README with:
- Quick start (binary, Docker, from source)
- API usage examples (curl for single, multi-TLD, bulk)
- CLI usage examples (check, bulk)
- Self-hosting guide
- Configuration reference (all 11 parameters)
- Architecture overview
- Contributing guidelines
- robots.txt (noindex result pages)
- Favicon and OG social card
File: README.md

title: P2: TLD List + Health + Metrics Endpoints
description:
GET /api/v1/tlds — return supported TLD list with count and bootstrap update timestamp
GET /health — return status (ok/degraded/unhealthy), bootstrap_age, uptime, checks_served
  Degraded: bootstrap >48h old. Unhealthy: bootstrap >7d or failed to load.
GET /metrics — Prometheus metrics (domcheck_requests_total, rdap_duration, cache_hits, etc.)
Package: internal/server/handlers_api.go

title: P6: Container Image Build + Registry Push
description:
Build and push Docker image to container registry for K8s deployment.
- GitHub Actions workflow: build on tag push, push to ghcr.io/jedarden/domain-check
- Multi-arch: linux/amd64 (apexalgo-iad runs amd64)
- Tag strategy: git tag → image tag (e.g., v1.0.0 → ghcr.io/jedarden/domain-check:v1.0.0)
- Also push :latest on main branch
File: .github/workflows/release.yml

title: P5: Load Testing Baseline
description:
Establish performance baselines and validate targets.
Targets: cached p99 <10ms, uncached p99 <2s, bulk p99 <5s, sustained 100req/s p99 <50ms
Tools: hey (smoke test), vegeta (precise rate testing)
Memory growth test: 50 req/s for 10 min, verify plateau (<100 MB growth)
Rate limiter verification: 200 req/s should trigger 429s
Document results in docs/benchmarks/

title: P1: RDAP Client
description:
HTTP client for querying RDAP registry servers directly.
- Query format: GET {rdap_server}/domain/{domain}
- Interpret HTTP 200 (taken), 404 (available), 429 (rate limited)
- Lenient JSON parser handling 8+ registry schema variations
- Normalize ldhName to lowercase (Verisign returns UPPERCASE)
- Handle empty body on 404 (Verisign)
- Parse dates with 0-6 fractional digits, Z and +HH:MM offsets
- Strip trailing dots from nameserver names
- Ignore unknown event types and custom conformance extensions
- Extract registrar, dates, nameservers, status from response
Package: internal/checker/rdap.go

title: P1: WHOIS Fallback Client
description:
WHOIS (port 43) fallback for ccTLDs without RDAP.
Major ccTLDs needing WHOIS: .de, .co, .jp, .kr, .cn, .ru, .se, .ch, .at, .be, .nz, .gg
- Use github.com/likexian/whois for queries + auto-discovery
- Use github.com/likexian/whois-parser for structured field extraction
- Return simplified result (available/taken + registrar if parseable)
- Handle WHOIS-specific rate limits (e.g., .de is aggressive)
Package: internal/checker/whois.go

title: P2: Bulk Check Endpoint
description:
POST /api/v1/bulk
- Accept JSON body: {domains: ['a.com', 'b.org', ...]}
- Max 50 domains per request, 64 KB max body (http.MaxBytesReader)
- Parallel execution via errgroup + per-registry semaphores
- 30s total timeout for bulk operation
- Return partial results: each domain has own success/error
- Error responses: 400 for >50 domains, empty array, invalid JSON; 413 for oversized body
Package: internal/server/handlers_api.go

title: P3: Home Page
description:
GET / — search form that works without JavaScript.
- Single text input: 'Enter a domain name'
- TLD checkboxes: .com, .org, .net, .dev, .io, .app
- Submit button, form action=/check
- Clean design, mobile-first, no clutter
- Works as plain HTML form submission (progressive enhancement added later)
Package: web/templates/index.html, internal/server/handlers_web.go

title: P1: Per-Registry Rate Limiter
description:
Rate limiting for upstream RDAP/WHOIS queries per registry.
- Token bucket via golang.org/x/time/rate
- Concurrency semaphores via golang.org/x/sync/semaphore
- Verisign: 10/sec, 10 concurrent
- PIR: 10/sec, 10 concurrent
- Google: 1/sec, 2 concurrent
- Default: 2/sec, 3 concurrent
- Exponential backoff on HTTP 429 (1s, 2s, 4s / 5s, 10s, 20s, 3 retries)
- Queue depth limit: 100 items per registry, service_busy after
Package: internal/checker/ratelimit.go

title: P4: DNS Pre-Filter
description:
Fast path optimization: skip RDAP for domains with active NS records.
- dig-equivalent NS lookup via net.LookupNS()
- If NS records found → definitely registered, return immediately (source: dns)
- If NXDOMAIN → proceed to RDAP for authoritative check
- Saves RDAP queries and rate limit budget for obvious cases
- ~0.1s per check vs ~0.1-0.7s for RDAP
Package: internal/checker/dns.go

title: P1: SSRF-Safe HTTP Client
description:
Prevent server-side request forgery in the RDAP/WHOIS client.
- Custom net.Dialer.Control callback blocking private IPs after DNS resolution
- Block: loopback, link-local, RFC 1918, ULA, zero network
- RDAP URL allowlist from IANA bootstrap (reject URLs not in bootstrap)
- Redirect validation: CheckRedirect verifies targets are in allowlist, max 3 redirects
- Input sanitization: reject domains containing /:@ before network requests
- HTTP client timeouts: 5s connect, 5s TLS, 10s response header, 15s total
Package: internal/checker/ssrf.go

title: T1: Fuzz Tests — ValidateDomain + ParseRDAPResponse
description:
Go 1.18+ native fuzz testing.
Target 1: FuzzValidateDomain — seed corpus of 9 inputs, properties: no panics, valid outputs have dots, labels ≤63, total ≤253
Target 2: FuzzParseRDAPResponse — seed from fixture files, properties: no panics, valid results have non-empty domain, deterministic
CI runs 30s per target. Crash cases saved to testdata/fuzz/ as permanent regression tests.
Package: internal/domain/parse_test.go, internal/checker/rdap_test.go

title: P3: Progressive Enhancement JS
description:
Vanilla ES6 JavaScript for enhanced UX (< 5 KB total).
- Debounced live search (after 3+ chars + TLD, 300ms debounce)
- Multi-TLD results expand inline without page reload (fetch API)
- Keyboard shortcuts: Enter to check, Tab to cycle TLDs
- Copy-to-clipboard for available domains
- Recent check history in localStorage (never sent to server)
- No build step, no framework
Package: web/static/app.js

title: T2: Integration Tests — API Endpoints (30 scenarios)
description:
Full middleware chain tests via httptest.NewRecorder with mock RDAP server.
7 single check tests: available, taken, empty, invalid, unsupported TLD, missing param, cached
3 multi-TLD tests: valid pair, partial failure, no TLDs specified
7 bulk tests: valid, max 50, over limit, empty, oversized body, invalid JSON, mixed results
4 rate limit tests: web limit, API limit, different IPs, Retry-After header
6 security tests: CSP, X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-Request-Id, CORS preflight, Content-Type
3 health check tests: normal, degraded, unhealthy
See plan.md API Integration Tests tables.
Package: internal/server/handlers_api_test.go

title: P3: Mobile-Responsive CSS
description:
Minimal CSS for mobile-first responsive design.
- Single file, no framework
- Mobile-first breakpoints
- Full-width input on mobile
- Color scheme for available (green) vs taken (red) results
- Clean typography, readable on all devices
- < 10 KB target
Package: web/static/style.css

title: T3: Web UI Tests (9 Playwright scenarios)
description:
End-to-end browser tests for the web interface.
1. Home page loads with search input
2. Empty form submission shows error
3. Valid domain without JS redirects to /check?d=..., shows result
4. Valid domain with JS shows inline result
5. Available domain shows green indicator
6. Taken domain shows red indicator + registration details
7. Multi-TLD checkboxes return results for multiple TLDs
8. Mobile viewport (390x844) — layout usable, input full-width
9. Result page URL is shareable (reload shows same result)
Requires Playwright + Go test server running.

title: P6: Traefik IngressRoute
description:
Expose domain-check via Traefik on apexalgo-iad.
- IngressRoute for HTTPS (TLS via cert-manager or Traefik default)
- Host: domaincheck.jedarden.com (or similar subdomain)
- Middleware: rate limiting at ingress level (supplementary to app-level)
- Strip prefix if needed
- Headers: X-Real-IP forwarding for trust-proxy
File: cluster-configuration/apexalgo-iad/domain-check/ingressroute.yaml

title: P6: Monitoring — ServiceMonitor
description:
Prometheus ServiceMonitor for scraping /metrics endpoint.
- Scrape interval: 30s
- Port: 8080, path: /metrics
- Labels matching Prometheus operator selectors on apexalgo-iad
- Enables domcheck_* metrics in Grafana dashboards
File: cluster-configuration/apexalgo-iad/domain-check/servicemonitor.yaml

title: P5: Dockerfile
description:
Multi-stage Docker build for minimal image.
Stage 1: golang:1.22-alpine — build the Go binary
Stage 2: alpine:3.19 — copy binary only
- Single binary with embedded assets (//go:embed)
- EXPOSE 8080
- CMD domain-check serve
- Health check: HEALTHCHECK CMD wget -q --spider http://localhost:8080/health
- Target image size: < 20 MB

title: P3: Embed Setup + HTML Templates
description:
//go:embed for templates and static assets, ensuring single-binary deployment.
- web/embed.go with embed.FS for templates/ and static/
- layout.html base template with CSP meta, favicon, CSS link
- template.ParseFS for loading from embedded FS
- fs.Sub + http.FileServer for static asset serving at /static/
Package: web/embed.go, web/templates/layout.html

title: P1: Result Types
description:
Core data types for domain check results.
- DomainResult: domain, available, tld, checked_at, source, cached, duration_ms, registration, error
- Registration: registrar, created, expires, nameservers, status
- Source enum: rdap, dns, whois, cache
- JSON serialization tags with omitempty for optional fields
Package: internal/domain/result.go

title: P3: Results Page
description:
GET /check?d=example.com — availability result display.
- Color coding: green = available, red = taken
- For taken domains: registrar, registration date, expiry, nameservers
- 'Also check' section: same name across other TLDs
- Shareable URL (bookmarkable)
- JSON link for API access to same result
- RDAP query time displayed
Package: web/templates/result.html, internal/server/handlers_web.go

title: Genesis: Domain Check Implementation
description:
## Genesis Bead
Tied to plan: /home/coding/domain-check/docs/plan/plan.md

## Overview
Authoritative domain availability checker with web UI + REST API, powered by RDAP.
Go backend, single binary, self-hostable, zero-tracking.

## Progress
- [ ] Phase 1: Core Engine
- [ ] Phase 2: API Server
- [ ] Phase 3: Web UI
- [ ] Phase 4: CLI Mode + Optimization
- [ ] Phase 5: Deployment + CI
- [ ] Phase 6: Kubernetes Deployment (apexalgo-iad)

title: P4: Configuration System
description:
Layered configuration: CLI flags > env vars > config file > defaults.
Library: github.com/peterbourgon/ff/v4
Env prefix: DOMCHECK_
11 parameters: addr, config, cache-size, cache-ttl-available, cache-ttl-registered,
bootstrap-refresh, trust-proxy, cors-origins, metrics, log-format, log-level
YAML config file via --config flag
Package: cmd/domain-check/main.go

title: P4: CLI Check Subcommand
description:
domain-check check <domain> [--tlds com,org,dev] [--format text|json|csv]
- Single domain check from command line
- Multi-TLD expansion with --tlds flag
- Output formats: text (human-readable), json, csv
- Uses same core engine as server
- Exit code 0 if available, 1 if taken, 2 on error
Package: cmd/domain-check/main.go (check subcommand)

title: P5: GitHub Actions CI Pipeline
description:
CI/CD pipeline for every push and PR.
- Go 1.22 + 1.23 matrix
- golangci-lint
- go test -race -coverprofile=coverage.out ./...
- Fuzz tests: 30s per target (ValidateDomain, ParseRDAPResponse)
- go build
- Codecov coverage upload
- No network calls to real RDAP servers (fixture-only)
File: .github/workflows/ci.yml

title: P2: Multi-TLD Check Endpoint
description:
GET /api/v1/check?d=example&tlds=com,org,dev,net,io
- Parse name and TLD list from query parameters
- Check each TLD in parallel (respecting per-registry rate limits)
- Return JSON array of results per TLD
- Partial success: individual TLDs can have errors while others succeed
Package: internal/server/handlers_api.go

title: P2: Security Middleware
description:
Security headers, CORS, body limits, request ID.
- CSP: default-src 'none'; script-src 'self'; style-src 'self'; img-src 'self'; form-action 'self'
- X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Referrer-Policy
- CORS: Access-Control-Allow-Origin configurable (default *), preflight handling
- Request ID: generate 16 hex chars or accept X-Request-Id from proxy
- Body size limits: http.MaxBytesReader on all POST handlers
Package: internal/server/middleware.go

title: P2: Single Check Endpoint
description:
GET /api/v1/check?d=example.com
- Parse and validate domain from query parameter
- Check cache → DNS pre-filter → RDAP/WHOIS
- Return JSON: domain, available, tld, checked_at, source, cached, duration_ms
- For taken: include registration object (registrar, dates, nameservers, status)
- Error responses: 400 invalid_domain, 400 unsupported_tld, 400 missing parameter
Package: internal/server/handlers_api.go

title: T1: Unit Tests — Domain Validation (48 cases)
description:
Table-driven tests for ValidateDomain().
22 valid inputs: basic, case, subdomains, multi-level TLDs, IDN, punycode, whitespace, max lengths
22 invalid inputs: empty, single label, empty label, hyphens, special chars, URLs, length limits, null bytes, emoji
4 edge cases: private PSL suffix, wildcard, invalid punycode, reserved names
See plan.md Testing section for full test case table.
Package: internal/domain/parse_test.go

title: P4: CLI Bulk Subcommand
description:
domain-check bulk <file> [--json] [--csv] [--concurrency 20]
- Read domain list from file (one per line)
- Parallel execution with configurable concurrency
- Output formats: text (streaming), json (collected), csv
- Progress indicator for large files
- Uses same core engine as server
Package: cmd/domain-check/main.go (bulk subcommand)

title: P1: Domain Input Validation
description:
Parse, validate, and normalize domain name inputs per RFC 1035/1123/5891.
- 7-step pipeline: trim → reject URL chars → IDN conversion → PSL TLD extraction → LDH regex → TLD verify → subdomain strip
- IDN via golang.org/x/net/idna (Lookup profile)
- Multi-level TLD via golang.org/x/net/publicsuffix (EffectiveTLDPlusOne)
- 253 char total limit, 63 char label limit, LDH rule enforcement
- Handle punycode, CJK, trailing dots, mixed case
Package: internal/domain/parse.go

title: P4: Parallel Bulk Execution
description:
Optimized concurrent execution for bulk domain checks.
- errgroup.SetLimit(50) for global concurrency cap
- Per-registry semaphores: Verisign=10, PIR=10, Google=2, default=3
- Group domains by registry for efficient rate limit utilization
- 30s total timeout for bulk operations
- Partial results: successful checks returned even if some fail
Package: internal/checker/checker.go (CheckBulk method)

title: T6: Validate Deployment — apexalgo-iad
description:
Validate the K8s deployment is working end-to-end.
Checks (read-only via kubectl-proxy):
1. Pod is Running: kubectl --server=http://kubectl-apexalgo-iad:8001 get pods -n domain-check
2. Health endpoint returns ok: curl http://<service>:8080/health
3. API returns valid results: curl http://<service>:8080/api/v1/check?d=google.com
4. Metrics endpoint accessible: curl http://<service>:8080/metrics
5. IngressRoute accessible externally: curl https://domaincheck.jedarden.com/health
6. Prometheus is scraping metrics (check targets in Prometheus UI)
Note: apexalgo-iad kubectl access is read-only via kubectl-proxy over Tailscale.

title: T5: Load Tests — Performance Targets
description:
Validate load testing targets are met:
1. hey -n 1000 -c 50 cached endpoint → p99 < 10ms
2. vegeta 100/s for 60s → p99 < 50ms, error rate < 0.1%
3. vegeta 200/s for 10s → 429 responses appear (rate limiter works)
4. Memory growth test: 50 req/s for 10 min → memory plateaus
All tests run against local server with mock RDAP backend.

title: P1: Result Cache
description:
In-memory LRU cache for domain check results.
- TTL: 5 min for available, 1 hour for registered, 30s for errors
- Bounded: max 10,000 entries with LRU eviction
- Thread-safe via RWMutex
- Cache key: normalized domain string
- ResultCache struct with Get(), Set(), methods
- Configurable TTLs and max size
Package: internal/checker/cache.go

title: P1: IANA Bootstrap Manager
description:
Load, cache, and refresh the IANA RDAP bootstrap file (data.iana.org/rdap/dns.json).
- Download on startup, refresh every 24h in background goroutine
- Thread-safe reads via RWMutex
- Map 591+ TLD entries to RDAP server URLs
- Fallback to hardcoded servers for .com/.net/.org if fetch fails
- BootstrapManager struct with Refresh() and Lookup(tld) methods
Package: internal/checker/bootstrap.go

title: P6: ArgoCD Application
description:
ArgoCD Application manifest for GitOps deployment.
- Source: cluster-configuration repo, path: apexalgo-iad/domain-check
- Destination: apexalgo-iad cluster, namespace: domain-check
- Sync policy: automated with prune and self-heal
- Never apply manifests directly with kubectl (GitOps via ArgoCD per CLAUDE.md)
File: cluster-configuration/apexalgo-iad/domain-check/application.yaml

title: P2: HTTP Server Setup
description:
Core HTTP server with net/http router and graceful shutdown.
- signal.NotifyContext for SIGINT/SIGTERM
- 15s shutdown drain for in-flight requests
- Route registration for all endpoints
- Middleware chain: request ID → logging → security headers → rate limit → CORS → handler
Package: internal/server/server.go, internal/server/routes.go

title: T1: Unit Tests — RDAP Parser (17 fixtures)
description:
Fixture-based tests for RDAP response parsing.
8 registered fixtures from: Verisign, PIR, Google, CentralNic, Identity Digital, Nominet, DENIC, Registro.br
4 available fixtures: Verisign (empty body), PIR (JSON), Google (JSON), CentralNic (objectClassName error)
5 error fixtures: rate limit, bad request, timeout, connection refused
4 WHOIS fixtures: .de + .jp registered/available
17 parser tests covering: case normalization, date formats, trailing dots, missing fields, unknown events
See plan.md RDAP Response Parser Tests table.
Package: internal/checker/rdap_test.go, testdata/rdap/

title: P2: Rate Limiting Middleware
description:
Per-IP rate limiting for client-facing endpoints.
- Web UI: 10 checks/min, API: 60/min, Bulk: 5 requests/min
- golang.org/x/time/rate with per-IP map
- Stale IP entry eviction every 10 minutes
- IP extraction: CF-Connecting-IP → X-Real-IP → X-Forwarded-For → RemoteAddr
- Configurable via --trust-proxy flag
- Return 429 with Retry-After header when exceeded
Package: internal/server/middleware.go
